title: Spent an evening actually using one of these "AI trading assistants" end to end instead of guessing whats under it
author: Salamandrine88
selftext:
Every platform ships an "AI trading assistant" now, so I spent an actual evening using one properly instead of forming an opinion from screenshots.

Used>!localtrade's (JexAI)!< for the session. The natural-language part genuinely works, you describe what you want, it builds the order and you confirm before it places, no fighting the UI. It pulls live data into the chat, has copytrader-matching and quick analysis buttons. One moment stuck with me though, I asked it for a read on something half expecting it to surface an angle I hadnt thought of, and it gave me a clean competent answer that was, when I actually looked, the same indicators I'd have pulled myself, just narrated back fast. Thats the pattern I kept hitting all night. Its a very good command-and-orchestration layer over stuff the platform already does, not the edge-generating brain people picture when they hear "AI".

The part I didnt expect to find more interesting than the trading side was the profiling, it visibly shifts what it suggests based on how youve been behaving, which is a real mechanic and probably the most genuinely "AI" thing in there, even if its closer to a recommender than anything else.

So for the builders and quants in here, since this is the right room for it: is anyone's assistant doing something structurally past NLP intent-parsing+orchestration+a personalization model? Real anomaly detection that holds up, genuine state across sessions, anything where the intelligence is structural and not a fast narrator over existing endpoints. I went in expecting to find the deep part and didnt, genuinely want to be pointed at one that has it.
